articles.title: Password Managers: The Free One Is Probably Enough
articles.metaTitle: Password Managers: The Free One Is Probably Enough
articles.metaDescription: What a password manager does, why the free versions are good enough for most UK households, and when paying two or three pounds a month is actually worth it.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Tools pillar now 2. Prices July 2026: Bitwarden free unlimited, family ~£40/yr for 6, 1Password ~£48/yr single no free tier, Proton Pass strong free tier. Recheck yearly. Differentiator: leads with the FREE recommendation, which review sites avoid because free pays no commission. Long term trust play.

earningPlan.detail: All three run affiliate programs. Lower per-signup than VPN (roughly £5-20) but recurring in some cases. Honest framing means fewer clicks but far better conversion from readers who genuinely upgrade to family plans.
The paid recommendation in this article is the family plan, which is where the commission actually is. Article recommends it only where it genuinely fits, which keeps it honest.

earningPlan.route: Companion article: how to check if your data was breached
earningPlan.detail: Have I Been Pwned angle. Same audience, natural next step, more affiliate surface for security tools.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: Selling Your Garden Produce in the UK: The Rules and the Money
articles.metaTitle: Selling Your Garden Produce in the UK: The Rules and the Money
articles.metaDescription: What you can legally sell from your garden or allotment in the UK, when you must register as a food business, and what surplus veg, eggs and jam actually earn.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Balances Side Hustles pillar (now 2). Key facts: primary vs processed split, <50 birds farm gate rule, eggs 28 day BB / 21 day sale, food business registration free + 28 days notice. Differentiator: honest money figures (£200-400/yr veg, £15-25/mo eggs) where competitors imply big income.

earningPlan.route: Seeds and garden supplies affiliate (Amazon UK, Suttons, Thompson & Morgan)
earningPlan.detail: Article ends by saying seedlings and plants sell better than veg. Natural link point for seed suppliers. UK garden retailers run affiliate programs open to small sites.

earningPlan.route: Preserving and labelling kit affiliate (jars, labels, thermometers via Amazon UK)
earningPlan.detail: Jam section needs jars and correct labels. Specific product need, high intent.

earningPlan.route: Digital product: UK food labelling template pack
earningPlan.detail: Allergen labelling is the part people get wrong. A simple compliant label template could sell for a few pounds, and doubles as an email list magnet.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: VPN in the UK: What You Actually Need and What You Do Not
articles.metaTitle: VPN in the UK: What You Actually Need and What You Do Not
articles.metaDescription: Are VPNs legal in the UK, what they really cost per month, when they are worth paying for, and the three situations where a VPN does nothing for you.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Fills Tools & Reviews pillar (last empty one). Prices July 2026: Surfshark ~£1.50, Nord ~£2.30, Proton ~£2.40 on long plans. RECHECK QUARTERLY, VPN prices move constantly. Differentiator: tells readers when NOT to buy one, and warns about renewal price jump.

earningPlan.route: VPN affiliate (NordVPN, Surfshark, Proton via Impact/PartnerStack)
earningPlan.detail: HIGHEST PAYING route on the whole site. UK VPN affiliates pay roughly £30-70 per signup, sometimes 100% of first term. All three named providers accept small publishers. Article is written honestly (says skip it if you do not need it), which protects trust while still qualifying for commission on readers who do.

earningPlan.route: Password manager affiliate (1Password, Bitwarden, Proton Pass)
earningPlan.detail: Natural companion article: password managers. Same audience, recurring commission, and genuinely useful advice.
Lower trust category with a lot of junk products. Only if a genuinely good UK-relevant option is found.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: Remote Jobs UK With No Degree: What Is Real and What Pays
articles.metaTitle: Remote Jobs UK With No Degree: What Is Real and What Pays
articles.metaDescription: Which UK work from home jobs genuinely hire without a degree, what they pay per hour, and how to spot the scams that target people looking for remote work.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Fills the empty Work & Career pillar. Pay £10-12/hr July 2026, recheck with National Living Wage changes each April. Scam section is the differentiator, competitors just list jobs.

earningPlan.detail: Article tells readers to rewrite CV per role. CV tools run affiliate programs with decent payouts and accept small sites. Strongest natural fit here.
Article frames these jobs as a doorway to better paid roles. Course affiliate links fit the progression angle honestly.
Some UK job boards pay per application or per signup. Needs traffic volume first.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: Coffee at Home: How Much a Daily Flat White Really Costs You
articles.metaTitle: Coffee at Home: How Much a Daily Flat White Really Costs You
articles.metaDescription: A shop flat white costs around £4.35 in the UK. The same drink at home costs about 40p. Here is the real yearly difference and the kit you actually need.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Costa flat white £4.35 July 2026, recheck yearly. Links to articles 2 and 4.

earningPlan.route: Amazon UK Associates (AeroPress, moka pot, frother)
earningPlan.detail: Article names exact kit under £50. Amazon UK Associates is open to small sites and approval is realistic now. Highest intent product mentions on the site so far.

UK bean subscriptions run affiliate or refer-a-friend schemes. Caveat: article tells readers to buy cheap supermarket beans, so only promote subscriptions honestly as the hobby upgrade, not the saving.
Article ends with where to put the saved money. Money comparison affiliates pay well but need traffic and FCA-aware wording. Later.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: Print on Demand UK: The Real Profit Per T-Shirt
articles.metaTitle: Print on Demand UK: The Real Profit Per T-Shirt
articles.metaDescription: What print on demand actually pays a UK seller once base cost, shipping, platform fees and VAT come out. Full worked example on a £25 t-shirt.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Printful raised some prices Feb 2026, recheck base costs quarterly. Links to articles 1 and 3.

earningPlan.detail: Both run affiliate programs paying a share of what referred sellers spend, often recurring for 12 months. Strong fit: this article names both suppliers. Printful pays on customer orders, Printify pays per signup plus revenue share.

earningPlan.route: Design tool affiliate (Canva Pro, Kittl, Placeit)
earningPlan.detail: POD sellers need design tools. Canva and Placeit run affiliate programs with recurring commission on subscriptions. Natural companion article: best design tools for POD.

earningPlan.route: Own POD shop as case study
earningPlan.detail: Open a small Etsy POD shop, publish monthly real revenue numbers. Case study content ranks well and builds trust no competitor can copy.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.

articles.title: Etsy Fees for UK Sellers: What You Really Pay Per Sale
articles.metaTitle: Etsy Fees for UK Sellers: What You Really Pay Per Sale
articles.metaDescription: Every fee Etsy charges UK sellers in 2026, with a worked example showing what a £25 sale really costs you after listing, transaction, processing and VAT.
articles.trackingNotes: Live. Regulatory fee 0.48% since 22 June 2026, recheck if Etsy announces changes. Internal links to articles 1 and 2.

earningPlan.detail: Etsy runs an affiliate program on Awin paying commission on purchases. Article can link handmade products or Etsy itself. Apply once site has 10+ articles.

earningPlan.route: Craft supplies and tools affiliate (Amazon UK Associates)
earningPlan.detail: Packaging, label printers, candle making kits mentioned in future companion articles. Amazon UK Associates is open to small sites.
Site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.
Sell or give free a fee calculator spreadsheet based on this article's maths. Could itself be sold on Etsy, which becomes a case study article.

articles.title: Best Cashback Credit Cards in the UK: What You Actually Earn
articles.metaTitle: Best Cashback Credit Cards UK: What You Actually Earn
articles.metaDescription: The real cashback rates on UK credit cards this year, the caps and fees nobody mentions, and how to work out which card pays you the most.
articles.trackingNotes: Live with cover photo. Rates need monthly recheck (Amex, Santander Edge change often).

earningPlan.route: Credit card affiliate (Awin / CJ UK finance programs)
earningPlan.detail: UK card issuers and comparison sites (TopCashback, Quidco, ClearScore) run affiliate programs via Awin and CJ. Payout per approved card application can be £20 to £60. Need: site with 10+ articles and traffic before applying, otherwise rejection likely.
Both have open refer-a-friend programs, no approval barrier. Natural fit inside this article as a bonus section: earn cashback on top of card cashback. Can add now.
Finance content has high ad rates (RPM often £10-25 UK). Apply after 20+ articles and some organic traffic, otherwise low earnings and risk of rejection.
Free PDF: card eligibility self-check list. Builds list for future affiliate promotions. Needs newsletter tool setup first.

articles.title: Side Hustle Tax Rules in the UK: When HMRC Needs to Know
articles.metaTitle: Side Hustle Tax Rules UK 2026: When HMRC Needs to Know
articles.metaDescription: Earning extra money in the UK? Here is exactly when you owe tax on a side hustle, what the £1,000 allowance covers, and what Vinted and eBay now tell HMRC.
articles.trackingNotes: Live with cover photo + internal links added 21 Jul. £3,000 threshold law status recheck monthly.

earningPlan.route: Accounting software affiliate (FreeAgent, QuickBooks UK, Coconut)
earningPlan.detail: UK bookkeeping tools for side hustlers pay per signup or trial. Natural fit: article already tells readers to keep records. FreeAgent and QuickBooks run programs via affiliate networks.
Self Assessment filing services pay per referred customer. Fits the register-by-5-October section perfectly.
Same as site-wide plan: after 20+ articles.
Free simple income/expense tracker download for email signup. Cheap to make, high perceived value for this audience.
